resources.string: One of your passes has new information
Point the camera at the pass\'s QR code
No app found to pick a file on this device

WhatPass: Your PKPASS digital card repository for Android

What is WhatPass?
WhatPass is an Android app designed to store, organize and manage digital cards in PKPASS format. Its goal is to offer an experience equivalent to the most advanced digital wallets, letting users always carry their cards, passes, coupons and credentials securely and up to date.

WhatPass turns your phone into a smart digital wallet capable of replacing paper or plastic cards with a modern, sustainable solution with advanced features.

1. What is a PKPASS file?

A PKPASS file is a digital card format originally used by Apple Wallet and adopted by many identification, loyalty and access-management systems.

Each PKPASS file can contain:

  • Unlimited information fields.
  • Data personalized for each user.
  • Images, logos and graphic elements.
  • QR codes, barcodes or Aztec codes for identification and validation.
  • Dynamic information that can update automatically.

    • Besides showing information, PKPASS cards can interact with the user through several mechanisms:

  • Sending push notifications.
  • Opening web links.
  • Sending cards via email, SMS, WhatsApp or other messaging services.
  • Accessing online services through personalized URLs.

    • PKPASS cards can also be used as loyalty cards. After scanning its QR code, the associated systems can grant or deduct points, update balances, register visits or modify any data on the card. These changes are automatically reflected on the digital card installed by the user.

    2. How does the PKPASS repository work?

    WhatPass acts as a centralized repository of digital cards. Its main functions are:

    Centralized storage
    All PKPASS cards are kept in a single app, avoiding the need to search through documents, emails or screenshots.

    Automatic updates
    When the issuer of a card makes changes, WhatPass can receive the updates and reflect them automatically on the device.

    Push notification reception
    Issuing organizations can send messages directly to installed cards to communicate promotions, reminders, schedule changes, event information or any other relevant communication.

    Card deletion
    Cards can be easily deleted when no longer needed.

    Notification and update management
    The user keeps full control of their data and can:

  • Turn automatic updates on or off.
  • Turn push notifications on or off.

    • Home-screen shortcuts
    WhatPass lets you create shortcuts on the device\'s home screen to quickly access a specific card without having to open the app first.

    Add event to calendar
    When a card includes a relevant date, you can add an event to the calendar.

    Locate on the map
    When a card includes a location, you can locate it in the device\'s map app.

    3. Practical examples of digital cards

    The PKPASS format is extremely versatile and can be used in many scenarios:

    Digital business cards
    Allow sharing up-to-date contact details, web links, email and professional networks.

    Membership cards
    Ideal for associations, sports clubs, cultural entities and professional organizations.

    Employee cards
    Make corporate identification and access to internal services easier.

    Customer or loyalty cards
    Allow accumulating and redeeming points, registering purchases and accessing personalized promotions.

    Discount coupons
    Businesses can distribute easily-updatable digital promotions.

    Event tickets and passes
    Access control via QR codes for concerts, conferences, fairs and cultural activities.

    Boarding passes
    Make access to flights and other means of transport easier without printing documents.

    How does the app work?

    WhatPass has been designed to make managing digital cards as simple as possible.

    Adding cards
    Cards can be added in three different ways:

  • Downloading a card from a URL.
  • Opening a PKPASS file.
  • Scanning a QR code.

    • Smart organization
    Once installed, cards are stored in WhatPass\'s internal repository, where they can be organized by different criteria:

  • Time order.
  • Urgency level.
  • Card type.

    • Quick access
    Users can create individual shortcuts on the device\'s home screen to open the most-used cards directly.

    Real-time communication
    WhatPass receives push notifications sent by card issuers, keeping information always up to date.

    Back-of-card information
    Cards include back-of-card information that supports hyperlinks to any URL. You can see the back-of-card information by tapping the arrow icon at the bottom of the front of the card.

    Replacing paper and plastic
    Digitizing cards and documents offers many advantages:

  • Reduces the use of paper and plastic.
  • Avoids losses and damage.
  • Allows remote updates.
  • Makes direct communication with the user easier.
  • Integrates messaging services and interactive links.
  • Includes loyalty programs with automatic point management.

    • Thanks to these capabilities, PKPASS digital cards are a modern, sustainable solution that\'s far more capable than traditional cards.

    Project history

    WhatPass was born after detecting a real need within the Android ecosystem.

    After confirming that no app offered, all together, the full set of features available in Apple Wallet, the idea came up to build a complete, open solution for Android.

    The PKPASS format is a standard created by Apple and requires meeting a series of technical specifications to ensure cross-platform compatibility. WhatPass has been developed respecting those standards to offer the highest possible compatibility and a consistent user experience.

    The project\'s goal is to bring all the advantages of modern digital wallets to Android users through a powerful, flexible and open-source app.

    WhatPass is based on the PassAndroid project, from which it inherits its original architecture.

    Legal notice and limitation of liability

    WhatPass is free software, distributed under the terms of the GNU General Public License version 3 (GPL-3.0-only). The complete source code is publicly available at github.com/glocalsaino/WhatPass, where anyone can review, modify and redistribute it under the same terms. Unless applicable law expressly states otherwise, the app is provided "as is" and "as available", without any express or implied warranty regarding its operation, availability, compatibility, continuity, freedom from errors or fitness for a particular purpose.

    Although the developers make reasonable efforts to ensure the quality and correct operation of the software, there is no guarantee that the app is free of errors, interruptions, security vulnerabilities or issues arising from interaction with third-party systems, devices or services.

    The user uses WhatPass at their own risk and is responsible for verifying that the use of the app is suitable for their needs and complies with applicable regulations. They are also responsible for the digital cards they install, the content stored in them, and the use they make of the app\'s features.

    The developers, contributors and distributors of WhatPass shall not be liable for damages or losses arising from the use of the app, the inability to use it, errors in the data contained in the digital cards, failures of third-party services, loss of information, or actions carried out by users or third parties through the cards managed by the app, except in cases where liability cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law.

    WhatPass acts solely as a storage and management tool for digital cards. The developers are not responsible for the content, authenticity, validity, legality or accuracy of PKPASS cards issued by third parties, nor for the communications, promotions, loyalty programs, points systems, events or services associated with such cards.

    Nothing in this legal notice shall limit or exclude any liability that, under applicable European Union or member-state law, cannot be legally excluded or limited.

    More information and privacy policy at whatpass.es. Contact: info@whatpass.es.
"Sorry, but there was a problem processing this card. If you want you can send me this card so I can check what the problem is and improve the software."
Importing the pass - please wait a bit
a card is shared with you
Do you really want to delete this card?
Tap the link in your email or website to add a pass\nor tap + to scan a QR code\nor open a .pkpass file from your device
You are about to add the expiration-date to the calendar - If you think there is a better date in this pass - please send it to me - WhatPass needs to know where this
Should I increase brightness when showing barcode?
Should I show more detail and less clutter?
How should I sort the passes?
Should I be dark or light?
demand esPass or Passbook from your vendor
This is imported from a image - not a real pass
This is imported from a PDF - not a real pass
Point the camera at the pass QR code
If the pass system requires an edit code, enter it here. Leave empty to scan without a code. You will not be asked again until you reopen the scanner.
Camera permission is required to scan passes
This QR code downloads a pass. Use \"Add pass by scanning a QR\" instead.

The beacon runs as a background service — you can close this screen safely. A notification will remain visible in the status bar while it is active. To stop it, use the notification action or return to this screen.
Major and Minor must be numbers between 0 and 65535
Enable Bluetooth to use the beacon
This device does not support BLE advertising
Bluetooth permission is required to broadcast
This device does not support BLE advertising
Too many active advertisers. Stop other apps and try again
